## [00:00 - 03:10]

Hi, this is Don Crowder. Let's talk about words versus images in your presentations. So when you think about this concept of one or the other, realize that too much of anything makes people bored. And so you always want to be jumping back and forth and giving people different ways of portraying the concepts that you're trying to say. So when you look at one versus the other, images are much easier on the eyes. Words are better at conveying information. It's all about creating a balance. Now, I realize that there are some of you who are very graphically oriented or who are fans of Steve Jobs kinds of presentations who say, Don, who are you to be talking about this, you use far too few images. And I totally agree. I would love to have unlimited time and unlimited budgets to have hundreds of images throughout all my presentations. We're going to talk about the expense of images in a little bit in this particular video. But also remember that there are real strong points to using words. So before I get into that, it's important to remember also that images are not just pictures. They also include things like videos and graphics, even other non standard ways of giving them texts. So for example, maybe having a series of boxes with arrows to illustrate step by step systems, even though someone could say, Well, that's text, that's a text slide. But just putting in those boxes and putting some color behind those boxes can turn it into a graphic, a step by step graphic. So all the experts tend to say, you should use images wherever possible. And you know what, I agree with them. But this whole concept of death by PowerPoint, I think is way overrated. And a lot of it has to do with the like 10 point text on PowerPoint slides. And you when you do it much more the way that I'm showing you here with bullet points, they hit the screen, you're trying to have it be your screen is regularly changing, ideally about once every eight seconds. And as I say that, I realize I haven't changed the slide and it's changed what's been what's appearing on your screen here for probably 30 seconds. And I feel sorry for that. Some people would even say virtually no words just use images like that. And that's great to do that. The problem of just using images is that words do a better job of conveying complex information, step by step information. And pictures are expensive, both to acquire because you can't just go on to Google images and grab any image you want to and put them in because you're just violated somebody's copyright and have potential for getting sued.

## [03:10 - 06:13]

But they're also expensive to build into a presentation, because if you got I can I can build this slide in about 20 seconds, writing three phrases. ## [09:26 - 11:19]

But now you can read the text. This is also a great way to bring your entire presentation into your color family.
